message:
revert: drop ensure_storage_cursor_row_layout early-return fast path

Reverts the early-return added in 34a61a0b. The premise of the
fast path — that when `!refreshed`, the row decode table is non-
empty, and the payload buffer covers the requested span, the layout
is already valid and the function has nothing to do — does not hold
in all the configurations where this hot loop is reached.

Specifically, callers that read multiple rows under a single cursor
pass and expect `eager_values_ready` to be reset by a fresh
ensure-call were depending on the slow path's reset side effects.
With the early return suppressing the slow path, those callers saw
stale eager-values state from a previous row and returned wrong
output for some cell types.

Restore the original always-fall-through shape. The redundant work
in the no-op case is the price of correctness — the fast path needs
either a more constrained guard (e.g. also check that no eager-
values were materialized for the previous row) or a different
caller-side cache before it can be reintroduced.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
